data.title: Healthcare among laggards in Q1; MRNA and DVA lead gainers while HUM drags
data.description:
The Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV) declined 5.29% in the first quarter of 2026; it slightly underperformed the broader S&P 500 Index, which fell 4.63% over the same period. Market sentiment suffered due to persistent inflation, rising Treasury yields, and an oil supply shock, pushing Brent prices over $100, peaking near $118 in late March, which hurt the healthcare sector's performance.

Performance across the 11 S&P 500 sectors has diverged notably, reflecting shifting sentiment amid geopolitical uncertainty. In the first quarter of 2026, the Energy Select Sector (XLE) increased by 37.02% year-to-date, outpacing the broader market, which saw the S&P 500 Index fall by 4.63%. While defensive sectors like Materials (XLB) and Utilities (XLU) remained positive. In contrast, the former leaders of the bull market—Technology (XLK) and Financials (XLF)—are entering Q2 deeply in the red.

The top S&P 500 healthcare gainers and losers for Q1 2026 are as follows:

Q1 top performers:

Moderna (MRNA) +63.55%

DaVita (DVA) +34.12%

Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) +17.17%

Quest Diagnostics (DGX) +13.62%

Merck & Co. (MRK) +12.20%

Q1 bottom performers:

Humana (HUM) -35.19%

Boston Scientific (BSX) -34.00%

Insulet Corporation (PODD) -26.82%

IQVIA Holdings (IQV) -26.29%

Waters Corporation (WAT) -22.76%

Industries performance:

The Health Care Equipment & Services sector declined 12.32% in Q1, while Pharmaceuticals, Biotechnology and Life Sciences are down 13.85% in Q1.

What Quantitative Measures Say

The Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV) has seen a severe downgrade in its Seeking Alpha Quant Rating, plunging from a 'Buy' (3.59/5) to a 'Sell' (1.3/5). This regression was driven by a dramatic collapse in momentum—which fell from a B to a D grade—even as liquidity remained a bright spot at A+. Meanwhile, the ETF's risk profile rating shifted slightly from D to D+.

What Analysts Expect

Investors can buy XLV or similar index funds like Vanguard’s VHT to get exposure to the healthcare sector. For those willing to take on more risk, RSPH is an equal-weighted option that offers more access to smaller companies, which can yield higher returns," said Seeking Alpha analyst Envision Research.

"For those seeking diversification, the Schwab US Dividend Equity ETF is a good option, with healthcare among its top exposures, likely increasing after its 2026 reconstitution. Investors may also look at individual stocks like JNJ, ABBV, and MRK," said the analyst.

On average, Seeking Alpha contributors see XLV as Hold.

Fund Flows

Despite a challenging quarterly return of -5.29%, investor conviction in the healthcare sector remained surprisingly resilient throughout the first three months of 2026. According to the latest flow data, the XLV ETF recorded net inflows of $1.11 billion between January 1 and March 30.

data.title: AI, chip stocks soar on hopes of resolution of Iran war
data.description:
Chip and AI-related stocks climbed on Wednesday amid hopes of a potential resolution to the Iran war.

U.S. President Donald Trump will deliver a prime-time address on Wednesday at 9 p.m. EST to provide an update on the war in Iran. Separately, Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, or IRGC, has reportedly threatened attacks on several U.S. technology companies operating in the Middle East, including Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), and Alphabet (GOOGL) (GOOG).

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) jumped about 1.4%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) rose about 1%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) climbed nearly 0.9%.

Shares of AI chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) rose about 1%, while Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) surged nearly 4%. Broadcom (AVGO) and Qualcomm (QCOM) each climbed nearly 1%.

Several other AI and networking-related stocks were also among the gainers. Lumentum (LITE) surged around 11%, while Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) and Coherent (COHR) each soared about 8%. Ciena (CIEN) jumped nearly 7%, while Corning (GLW) grew about 6%. Celestica (CLS) jumped about 5%, Arista Networks (ANET) climbed around 3%, and Cisco (CSCO) rose about 2%.

Micron Technology (MU) soared about 11%, while Intel (INTC) — which announced that it is buying back a 49% equity interest in a joint venture related to its Fab 34 facility in Ireland from Apollo for $14.2B —  surged nearly 10%. Marvell Technology (MRVL) jumped about 7%, and  Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) grew nearly 4%. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) rose about 3%, Analog Devices (ADI) climbed around 2%, while Arm (ARM), GlobalFoundries (GFS), and Texas Instruments (TXN) each gained nearly 1%. 

Chip equipment makers: ASML (ASML), Applied Materials (AMAT), and Lam Research (LRCX) each surged about 5%, while KLA (KLAC) soared nearly 4%.

data.title: Eli Lilly gains approval of weight loss pill orforglipron
data.description:
• The US FDA has approved Eli Lilly's weight loss pill, the GLP-1 receptor agonist Foundayo (orforglipron).
  • Approval was based on the results of the phase 3 ATTAIN-1 and ATTAIN-2 trials. The first trial compared three dosage strengths of orforglipron. Patients taking the drug in the 72-week trial saw an average weight loss of 27.3 pounds (12.4%) compared to 2.2 pounds (0.9%) with placebo.
  • Lilly said it is currently accepting prescriptions through its LillyDirect website with Foundayo shipping starting on April 6. Shortly after, it will be widely available through pharmacies and telehealth providers.
  • Through LillyDirect, the starting monthly price for Foundayo is $149 for cash pay customers and $25 for those with commercial insurance.  
  • Foundayo will compete against Novo Nordisk's (NVO) Wegovy (semaglutide) pill, which hit the market in January starting at $149 a month for self-pay customers.
  • Trials found that those on the Wegovy pill lost an average of 35 pounds (~15%) over 68 weeks.
  • Lilly shares are up ~5% in Wednesday trading.

data.title: SpaceX files confidentially for IPO - Bloomberg
data.description:
SpaceX (SPACE) filed confidentially for an initial public offering.

The rocket, satellite, and AI company submitted its draft IPO registration to the US Securities and Exchange Commission, according to a Bloomberg report on Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The filing puts SpaceX (SPACE) on schedule for a June listing.

SpaceX (SPACE) could look for a valuation of more than $1.75 trillion, Bloomberg has previously reported. An IPO for SpaceX would raise as much as $75 billion.

SpaceX didn’t immediately respond to a Bloomberg request for comment.

Musk is said to be talking about allocating as much as 30% of SpaceX's IPO to individual investors, at least three times as much as in a typical IPO.

data.title: Utah man files class action lawsuit against Perplexity for sharing search data with Google, Meta
data.description:
A Utah man is part of a class action lawsuit filed against Perplexity, Meta Platforms (META), and Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) alleging that the companies violated privacy laws.

The civil suit, filed on Tuesday in the U.S. Northern District Court of California in San Francisco, alleges that conversations users had with Perplexity's AI Machine search engine were shared with Meta and Google for advertising purposes without consent. The suit also alleged that information was shared even when users were engaged in Perplexity's “Incognito” mode. 

"The companies that deal in this data—such as Defendants Meta and Google—are some of the largest companies in the world," the suit reads. "When these companies gain access to individuals' intimate, personal data (such as the data that Plaintiff shared here), they are able to exploit this sensitive data for their own benefit, including targeting individuals with advertising and reselling their sensitive data to additional third parties."

"No reasonable person would have expected that Perplexity would share complete transcripts of their conversations with Perplexity's AI Machine with companies like Meta and Google," it said. "But that is what Perplexity did … In return for sharing all this sensitive data with Meta and Google, Perplexity received advertising and analytics benefits that it used for its own benefit."

The primary plaintiff, who goes by the alias John Doe in the suit, alleged Perplexity's AI Machine shared personal information about "his family's finances, his tax obligations, his investment portfolio, and his investment strategies." 

Perplexity indicated it was unaware of the 140-page civil suit.

"We have not been served any lawsuit that matches this description, so we are unable to verify its existence or claims," said Jesse Dwyer, Chief Communications Officer of Perplexity, in an email to Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Sensient is called one of the biggest beneficiaries of the switch to natural color ingredients
data.description:
Sensient Technologies Corporation (SXT) rallied on Wednesday after UBS launched coverage of the stock with a Buy rating. 

Analyst Joshua Spector called Sensient Technologies (SXT) the most natural growth opportunity in the food ingredients sub-sector.

Spector and his team believe that U.S. grocers and food producers are on the verge of a large shift from synthetic food dyes to natural color ingredients. Against that backdrop, Sensient Technologies (SXT) stands as the largest producer of natural colors, with ~60% sales exposure to North America. 

Natural color adoption is expected to increase in the second half of this year to meet customer conversion targets at the start of 2027. "This could drive a >2x increase in SXT's color group sales by 2030e (~17% CAGR). We don't see this growth priced into the stock, trading at only a slight premium to avg despite our outlook for >2x historical growth," highlighted Spector. 

The firm's 2027 sales and EBITDA estimates on SXT are above consensus, which leads it to see the stock as undervalued. UBS' price target of $115 represents more than 25% upside potential.

Shares of Sensient (SXT) ripped a 6.0% gain in late Wednesday morning trading.
